highlights.text: I wrote Thursday at 4pm: “My general lean is after today’s pullback ES can try some backtesting.
My general lean is bulls need to hold 6843 ideally.
There are many of these, but the foundation of them is what I call the two siblings: Elevator down (sibling 1) , into Failed Breakdown, into short squeeze (sibling 2) .
Elevator Down refers to vertical red candles where no support matters.
These short squeezes are caused by one thing: My core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
ES went elevator down 50 points rapidly to 6808.
In the morning we went elevator down 70+ points down to 6791.

I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “If this is the case, the obvious entry is to wait for 6795 to defend (doesn’t need to be exact) then recover today’s 6808 daily low.” Yesterday morning ES flushed down to 6791, recovered 6808, and we ripped hard to 6860+ again.
I wrote yesterday: “In summary for tomorrow: ES put in a big Failed Breakdown today by trapping under Friday’s 6808 low down to 6791, and recovering 6808.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.
In doing so, ES lost Friday’s daily low at 6808.

post_title: Two Big Green Days For SPX. More Incoming? Feb 19 Plan
